orchestkit · session state · live mermaid

What's going on
the whole thread, in three diagrams

Everything from this session, rendered. Two PRs, one real decision still open. Tabs below.

Everything that happened, and where it landed

Green = merged. Blue = open and ready. Amber = waiting on you.

flowchart TD
  S(["This session"]) --> H["Eval harness was lying:
reported PASS for runs it never made"]
  S --> A["Catalog audit:
114 skills, 36 agents"]
  H --> H1["4 vacuous-verdict fixes"]:::done
  H --> H2["budget governor"]:::done
  H --> H3["3 new zero-cost gates"]:::done
  H1 & H2 & H3 --> P1(["PR merged:
honest verdicts + gates"]):::done
  A --> A1["36 gaps fixed"]:::done
  A --> A2["docs + gate register"]:::done
  A2 --> P2(["PR 3054 merged"]):::done
  A1 --> P3(["PR 3056 open,
ready to merge"]):::open
  A --> R["ratchets burned down"]:::done
  R --> R1["model recency 37 to 0"]:::done
  R --> R2["collisions 7 to 0"]:::done
  R --> R3["unreachable 15 to 1"]:::done
  R3 --> D{{"but: made 11 skills
slash-invocable"}}:::wait
  D --> DEC(["OPEN: token cost
34 to 45 commands"]):::wait
  classDef done fill:#1e4633,stroke:#3fc088,color:#d3f4e4;
  classDef open fill:#223a4d,stroke:#47a8f0,color:#d1e9fb;
  classDef wait fill:#4d4022,stroke:#f0b93d,color:#f5e6c4;
2
PRs merged
1
PR ready
4
gates added ($0)
1
decision open

Two gates pull opposite ways

This is why the reachability fix was not obviously right. One gate wants a skill reachable; another caps how many may be.

flowchart LR
  U["a skill reachable
by NOBODY"]:::bad --> A["make it
user-invocable: true"]:::ok
  U --> B["grant it to
an agent"]:::info
  A --> I{{"island gate:
has an automated
trigger path?"}}
  A --> C{{"token cap 34:
50-100 tokens
each, every session"}}
  B --> I
  I -->|no| X["FAIL:
allowlist with a reason"]:::bad
  I -->|yes| PASS1["pass"]:::ok
  C -->|over| Y["FAIL:
raise cap, costs
every session forever"]:::bad
  C -->|under| PASS2["pass"]:::ok
  classDef ok fill:#1e4633,stroke:#3fc088,color:#d3f4e4;
  classDef bad fill:#512622,stroke:#db5a4f,color:#f5c9c7;
  classDef info fill:#223a4d,stroke:#47a8f0,color:#d1e9fb;
The trap: I said flipping skills to user-invocable was free. The disable-model-invocation: true part keeps descriptions out of context, which is true. But each slash command still costs ~50-100 tokens, and the count is hard-capped. My change jumps it 34 to 45, on a session budget already ~1500 tokens over.

The one open call: which of the 11 survive?

Every skill I flipped, and whether it earned its slot. Evidence = does its own SKILL.md tell a user to type /ork:name?

flowchart TD
  ELEVEN(["11 skills flipped
to slash-invocable"]) --> Q{{"does its own doc
say ork name ?"}}
  Q -->|"yes 14x, 12x"| BUG["4 broken contracts:
feedback, skill-evolution,
configure, upgrade-assessment"]:::bug
  Q -->|"argument-hint only"| HINT["2: presentation-builder,
release-checklist"]:::hint
  Q -->|"no signal at all"| NONE["5: ascii-visualizer, audit-skills,
checkpoint-resume, notebooklm,
validate-counts"]:::none
  BUG --> KEEP(["KEEP
they promised a command
that did not exist"]):::ok
  HINT --> MAYBE(["judgment call"]):::hint
  NONE --> DROP(["DROP
nobody has missed them,
budget is over"]):::bad
  classDef bug fill:#512622,stroke:#db5a4f,color:#f5c9c7;
  classDef hint fill:#4d4022,stroke:#f0b93d,color:#f5e6c4;
  classDef none fill:#2d323a,stroke:#667085,color:#9099a8;
  classDef ok fill:#1e4633,stroke:#3fc088,color:#d3f4e4;
  classDef bad fill:#512622,stroke:#db5a4f,color:#f5c9c7;
My recommendation: keep the 4 broken contracts (+200-400 tokens, in line with past cap raises), drop the 7 that no evidence supports. Cap goes 34 to 38 instead of 45.
